title: Does Yamagas statement about EoTVvsEoE affect concurrency
display_title: Does Yamagas statement about EoTVvsEoE affect concurrency
wikitext:
[]: Does Yamaga's statement about EoTVvsEoE affect concurrency?             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 07, 2010 8:54 pm Recently, Yamaga stated that EoTV was the intended ending. Unless you haven't been to this site in a month, you know that. We all do. So now the question is: Was EoE supposed to be concurrent with EoTV? This is not asking if they are thematically(sp?) concurrent. They are. This is not asking if Shinji accepted or denied Instrumentality. It's ambiguous. This is not asking if Rei has Lilith's soul in the TV ending. She does. I've seen enough concurrency discussions derail into the above. Also, if, like planned, there never was EoE, how would we explain the first bookend Rei?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 07, 2010 8:58 pm Wasn't this pretty well discussed already in the original thread made for it? :-\

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 07, 2010 9:01 pm I read the original thread, and don't remember anything of the sort.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 07, 2010 9:32 pm Concurrency is an entirely fan-made concept and was illegitimate long before this statement. In fact, in the RCB that accompanied EoE's release the theory is ruled out: There is one ending as shown in TV episodes 25 and 26, while episodes 25' and 26' as shown in "THE END OF EVANGELION" are another ending.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 07, 2010 9:37 pm I'm not sure how that RCB statement proves anything for whether or not they're concurrent. EoTV and EoE are both endings, yes, and they are not the same work. :-\

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 07, 2010 10:39 pm Seems pretty self-evident to me. over here is an ending, and this over here is a another ending. As in not the same. Different. I don't feel like digging through it again, but there's also a part where they say that 25' is based on the original script but 26' is completely new. Oh, and the director of 25' (name escapes me at the moment) also says in the interview section that he thinks they should have just stopped with the broadcast TV version rather than redo it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 07, 2010 10:44 pm "Different" doesn't mean or imply "not concurrent." They're pretty clearly different. If they were the same work, there wouldn't be any possibility for debate over whether or not they were concurrent or similar to each other, and we'd call them both the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 07, 2010 10:54 pm Basically, the comment is there to tell the audience to just pretend 25 & 26 didn't happen. I get the feeling that you want it to literally say 'nope, not concurrent'... but why would the theatrical book explicitly deny an interpretive theory that never occurred to the producers in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 07, 2010 10:58 pm So no concurrency was proved in the most roundabout way possible, so none told notice. I knew it! But again, first bookend Rei in TV continuity?? Actual explanation for the bookend Reis in EoE continuity?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jul 07, 2010 11:01 pm NemZ wrote: Basically, the comment is there to tell the audience to just pretend 25 & 26 didn't happen. I get the feeling that you want it to literally say 'nope, not concurrent'... but why would the theatrical book explicitly deny an interpretive theory that never occurred to the producers in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jul 07, 2010 11:09 pm So there's no Tier 1-3 material which explicitly states a common ground for concurrency between EoTV and EoE, is that it?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:50 am Does it help to know I WAS going to ask Yamaga about concuurency but decided to have redundent redundency instead? That said, no, concurrency or not is NOT affected. Renewal/Platinum booklets, RCB, and Refrain of Evangelion CD booklet at least say the endings have the same theme(s) executed differently, although no statement on concurrency is given either way . I'll pull up quotes tomorrow. AND I'LL ASK YAMAGA NEXT YEAR! @ self.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 15, 2010 2:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I'll pull up quotes tomorrow.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Sep 15, 2010 3:51 pm Aside from the Evangelion project booklet or whatever it is, is there any record or plan of what the last two episodes were going to be like? I know that "Air" is pretty close to what was originally intended, and I think that the director's cut of Episode 24 has an animatic of "Air" at the end, in the format of the previews at the end of every episode, but what about Episode 26? Were scripts even written for what those two episodes were going to be originally?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 15, 2010 4:50 pm If anyone can read those quotes and not see that concurrency is a misguided theory then I don't know what else could possibly be said on the matter.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Sep 15, 2010 7:06 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: I think that the director's cut of Episode 24 has an animatic of "Air" at the end, in the format of the previews at the end of every episode

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Sep 15, 2010 7:15 pm Based off SSD-chan's suggestions in some other thread, I set up two DVD players and switched channels between the TV ending and EoE and it all made perfect sense. One was external and the other was internal. I really don't care what the creators say. If a certain interpretation of their work stands to reason it stands. That being said, I only watched episode 26 of the TV series up until a certain point. I guess my point is that inside my brain everything makes sense. Except when it doesn't. SO THERE. I made the two endings work. My perception is reality.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Sep 15, 2010 9:19 pm The first quote said that no concurrency is canon. The second quote said the same. The third quote suggested weak concurrency, but said NC and SC are possibilities. The fourth said strong concurrency. All four sources appear second tier, so I'll go by majority. No concurrency = 2 1/4 Weak concurrency = 1/2 Strong concurrency = 1 1/4 No concurrency wins. Any objections? Now, onto the Reis....

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Sep 15, 2010 9:35 pm EvangelionFan wrote: I believe this preview was added to 24' with the intention that one watch 25' directly after it. Sadly, As ADV only ever had the rights to the series while Mangle had the rights to the films, there is yet to be an English release that includes both EoTV and EoE. So for English viewers without a seperate copy of EoE, the preview of 'Air' at the end of 24' is like a message-in-a-bottle.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 15, 2010 11:17 pm TehDonutKing wrote: The fourth said strong concurrency.
